tropical_rainforest.name: Tropical Rainforest
tropical_rainforest.desc: The most biodiverse ecosystems on Earth. Dense canopy, year-round growth, massive carbon stocks in biomass.

tropical_dry_forest.name: Tropical Dry Forest
tropical_dry_forest.desc: Seasonal forests with distinct wet/dry cycles. Often overlooked but critical for local carbon and biodiversity.

mangrove.name: Mangrove
mangrove.desc: The most carbon-dense ecosystems on Earth. They store carbon in waterlogged soil for millennia.

temperate_deciduous.name: Temperate Deciduous
temperate_deciduous.desc: Seasonal forests that shed leaves annually. Moderate carbon storage with rich soil carbon pools.

temperate_coniferous.name: Temperate Coniferous
temperate_coniferous.desc: Pine and fir forests of the Mediterranean and northern climates. Resilient but vulnerable to fire.

boreal_forest.name: Boreal Forest
boreal_forest.desc: The world's largest land biome. Slow-growing but massive total carbon stock across vast areas.

grassland_savanna.name: Grassland / Savanna
grassland_savanna.desc: Open landscapes with deep root systems. Store carbon primarily below ground.

wetland_peatland.name: Wetland / Peatland
wetland_peatland.desc: Peat accumulates over thousands of years. The densest carbon stores per hectare on land.

seagrass_meadow.name: Seagrass Meadow
seagrass_meadow.desc: Underwater meadows that sequester carbon 35x faster than tropical rainforests per area.

agricultural_cropland.name: Agricultural Cropland
agricultural_cropland.desc: Depleted soils with minimal carbon. Monocultures that replace diverse ecosystems.

degraded_bare_land.name: Degraded / Bare Land
degraded_bare_land.desc: Eroded, lifeless land. Almost no carbon storage. This is what we're fighting to restore.

urban_built.name: Urban / Built
urban_built.desc: Concrete and asphalt. Heat islands with minimal biological carbon cycling.
